title: 95. Unique Binary Search Trees II
question:
Given an integer n, generate all structurally unique BST's (binary search trees) that store values 1 ... n.

Example:

Input: 3
Output:
[
  [1,null,3,2],
  [3,2,null,1],
  [3,1,null,null,2],
  [2,1,3],
  [1,null,2,null,3]
]
Explanation:
The above output corresponds to the 5 unique BST's shown below:

   1         3     3      2      1
    \       /     /      / \      \
     3     2     1      1   3      2
    /     /       \                 \
   2     1         2                 3
code: # Definition for a binary tree node.\u000A# class TreeNode(object):\u000A#     def __init__(self, x):\u000A#         self.val \u003D x\u000A#         self.left \u003D None\u000A#         self.right \u003D None\u000A\u000Aclass Solution(object):\u000A    def generateTrees(self, n):\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        :type n: int\u000A        :rtype: List[TreeNode]\u000A        \u0022\u0022\u0022\u000A        lst \u003D range(1, n + 1)\u000A        if not n: return []\u000A        def clone(node):\u000A            if not node: return\u000A            new_node \u003D TreeNode(node.val)\u000A            new_node.left \u003D clone(node.left)\u000A            new_node.right \u003D clone(node.right)\u000A            return new_node\u000A        def _generateTrees(i, j):\u000A            if i \u003E\u003D j: return [None]\u000A            res \u003D []\u000A            for k in range(i, j):\u000A                left \u003D _generateTrees(i, k)\u000A                right \u003D _generateTrees(k + 1, j)\u000A                for l in left:\u000A                    for r in right:\u000A                        node \u003D TreeNode(lst[k])\u000A                        node.left \u003D clone(l)\u000A                        node.right \u003D clone(r)\u000A                        res.append(node)\u000A            return res\u000A        return _generateTrees(0, len(lst))
